w:r.w:t: Customer Experience • Menu UX • Order Lifecycle • Driver Management • Admin & Operations • v3.5 — March 2026
Goal: convert first-time visitors into orders with minimum friction. Every section has one job.
Right side. Shows "Log In" when logged out; shows name or avatar when logged in.
Right side. Shows item count badge + running subtotal. Hidden when cart is empty.
Full-width hero with brand tagline and two primary CTAs.
"Premium wings. Signature sauces. Crispy every time. No excuses."
Primary CTAs: two equal-weight buttons — Delivery and Pickup. Clicking either opens the ordering mode panel (see Section 2).
Button: "See the Menu" → navigates to Menu page
Privacy policy + Terms of service links
Allergen disclaimer: general warning that menu items may contain allergens.

Before showing the menu, the customer must declare their fulfillment mode (Pickup or Delivery), schedule, and (for delivery) their address. This information is locked in for the session and shown persistently in the menu header. Terminology note: the customer-facing UI may use “order type” as a friendly label, but all API payloads, analytics, admin filters, and code must use the canonical field name fulfillment_type (PICKUP | DELIVERY). Never use “order type” in backend code or data — it was deprecated in Section 10.5 because it conflated fulfillment and source. Canonical field mapping for all teams: Display label = “order type” (UI copy only); Canonical field = fulfillment_type (DTOs, DB columns, analytics labels, admin filters). This mapping must be enforced in code review — do not let the friendly display label leak into any data layer.
Triggered by: clicking the "Pickup" CTA on the homepage or changing order mode from the menu header.
• Pickup time selector (dropdown or time picker)
• Minimum lead time: 30 minutes from current time
• Available time slots must respect store operating hours
• Default pre-selected time: next available slot (current time + 30 min, rounded to next 5-min interval)
• On confirm: user proceeds to Menu page with pickup context locked in
Triggered by: clicking the "Delivery" CTA on the homepage or changing order mode from the menu header.
• Delivery address input with autocomplete (Google Places API recommended)
• Contactless delivery preference (Hand to me / Leave at door / Call on arrival / Text on arrival)
• Buzzer / gate code / unit number field
• Minimum lead time: same as pickup (30 minutes) unless updated by owner
• On confirm: user proceeds to Menu page with delivery context locked in
📌 Delivery Zone — MVP Rule (owner must configure before launch):
Full delivery zone map enforcement is a near-term feature. For MVP, use a postal code allowlist.
Admin configures a list of accepted postal codes in location_settings.allowed_postal_codes.
At address entry: system checks the entered postal code against the allowlist.
If outside the list: show error "Sorry, we don't currently deliver to that area." before the customer builds a cart.
Do NOT let the customer reach checkout before discovering the address is not serviceable.
Owner must provide the initial postal code list before launch. Default: empty list = delivery disabled.
"Delivery to 1544 Dundas St — Today at 7:00 PM [ Change ]"

"Change" re-opens the ordering mode panel. If the customer changes mode after adding items to the cart, display a warning: "Changing your fulfillment mode will keep your cart items — prices and availability will be rechecked."
🔄 Cart Revalidation After Mode Change — Required
After any fulfillment_type change (PICKUP → DELIVERY or DELIVERY → PICKUP), the backend must re-run:
• Item availability — some items may be delivery-only or pickup-only
• Modifier validity — modifier groups may differ by fulfillment type
• Promo eligibility — promos have eligible_fulfillment_type; pickup-only promos must be removed when switching to delivery
• Delivery fee / tip / prepayment logic — tip and delivery fee only apply to DELIVERY
• Scheduling validity — the new fulfillment type may have different available time slots
If any promo, item, or modifier is no longer valid after the mode change:

→ Remove invalid items and modifiers from the cart server-side, but NEVER silently — the backend must return a structured revalidation diff listing every change: items removed (with reason), modifiers dropped, prices updated. The UI must display this diff clearly to the customer before they proceed, so they know exactly what changed and can confirm or abandon the cart.
→ Show a banner for removed promos: "Your promo code is not valid for [new mode] orders."
→ Recalculate totals before showing the updated cart to the customer
This revalidation must happen server-side. Never trust the frontend to apply it correctly.
Desktop: sticky left-side category navigation + scrollable item grid on the right
Mobile: horizontal scrollable category tab bar pinned at top + items below
Clicking a category tab scrolls the item list to that category (Uber Eats / Skip-style behaviour)
Each menu item is displayed as a card. Cards contain:
Item image (uploaded by admin — mandatory for launch)
Short description: highlights product quality, preparation method, and key ingredients
Heat level indicator: e.g. 🌶️ Mild / 🌶️🌶️ Medium / 🌶️🌶️🌶️ Hot / 🌶️🌶️🌶️🌶️ Fire
Tapping or clicking a card opens the Item Customization flow
A search bar at the top of the menu page filters item cards in real time by name and description.
📌 Menu Data Normalization — Required Before Admin Entry

Do not copy raw menu board text directly into the admin panel. Before data entry, normalize the menu source: store each wing flavour as a canonical row in wing_flavours with heat_level tag (MILD, MEDIUM, HOT, DRY_RUB, PLAIN). Map every item to exactly one menu_categories row. Express house specials, lunch specials, and combo logic as structured product rules using menu_item_schedules and modifier_groups — not as duplicated raw strings or prose descriptions. Deduplication of flavour names across contexts (wings builder, combos, sides) must happen during
normalization, not after import. If the same sauce appears under two names in the source material, pick one canonical name and use it everywhere.
Applies to: Burgers, Chicken Tenders, Wraps, Poutines, Specialty Fries, Appetizers, and all non-wing items. Wings Builder (Section 5) and Combos Builder (Section 6) have their own flows.
Item image — full-width banner at top
Sticky bottom bar: quantity selector ( − / + ) + "Add to Cart" + live price
• All ingredients shown as toggles. Default state: included (ON).
• Tap any ingredient to exclude it. Toggle switches to removed state.
• Removed ingredients shown with strikethrough / greyed-out styling.
• No limit on how many ingredients can be removed.
• Printed clearly on kitchen receipt / KDS so staff can prepare accordingly.
Ingredients: Flour tortilla, mixed greens, roasted red peppers, cucumber, tomato, red onion, roasted garlic, hummus, feta cheese, lemon vinaigrette
Customer de-selects: Roasted garlic, Lettuce (mixed greens)
Cart shows + kitchen prints: "Veggie Wrap — No roasted garlic, No lettuce"
Required selections highlighted in orange with rule text ("Choose 1", "Choose up to 2", etc.)
Optional selections shown in neutral style
Radio buttons for single-select; checkboxes for multi-select
Subtext: "We'll do our best — special instructions are not guaranteed."
"Add to Cart" active only when all required selections are complete
If required selection missing on tap: auto-scroll to first incomplete section, highlight with orange border + "Selection required" label
4.6 Add-Ons for Wraps, Burgers, Poutines & Salads

In addition to ingredient removal (Section 4.2), customers can add optional extras to applicable items. Add-ons are priced individually and displayed as a separate section in the customization flow, clearly below the ingredient removal toggles.
• Each add-on shows: name + price delta (e.g. "Extra cheese +$1.00")
• Checkboxes — customer can select multiple add-ons
• No minimum selection required — entirely optional
• Price updates in real time as add-ons are toggled
• Selected add-ons are printed on kitchen receipt and shown on KDS ticket
• Add-ons are stored as modifier_options linked to a modifier_group with is_required = false
Admin configures available add-ons per item from the menu management panel. Add-ons reuse the existing modifier_groups / modifier_options schema — no separate table required.
The Wings Builder is the most complex and most important UX component on the site. Step-by-step guided flow on one scrollable page with a progress indicator at the top.
Menu shows a single "Wings (By the Pound)" card. Tapping it opens the Wings Builder.
Card description: "Choose your style, preparation, weight, flavours, and extras."
Wing Type → Preparation → Quantity → Flavour(s) → Saucing Method → Extra Flavour → Add-ons → Instructions
Choose one. Always the first question.

Shown only when the selected wing type supports multiple preparation options. Bone-In: customer chooses Breaded or Non-Breaded. Boneless: Non-Breaded only — Step 2 is auto-selected and skipped in the UI (no user input needed).
Price updates immediately. Selected weight determines flavour slot count.
• 1 lb • 1.5 lb • 2 lb • 3 lb • 4 lb • 5 lb
When 5 lb selected: UI shows "You'll need to choose exactly 3 flavours and a saucing method."
Flavour slot count determined by weight in Step 3. All slots must be filled to proceed.
1. Select a heat category (expands to show sauces in that category)
2. Select a specific sauce within that category
• No Flavour (Plain) — always visible, first-class selection, never hidden
• Slots 2 and 3 only appear when required by weight
• Each slot is independent — same sauce can be chosen more than once
Hidden and skipped for single-flavour orders. Required when 2 or 3 flavours selected.
Label: "How would you like your sauces applied?"
A) Half & Half [ Default pre-selected ] — half in Flavour 1, half in Flavour 2
B) Mixed Together — both sauces on all wings
C) Sauce on the Side — one tossed, one served separately
Sub-question: "Which flavour goes on the side?"
All 3 flavours collected in Step 4 first. Then distribution is selected.
Label: "How would you like your 3 flavours distributed?"
A) Two Mixed + One on the Side [ Default pre-selected — most kitchen-friendly ]
Sub-question: "Which flavour do you want on the side?"
The other two are automatically mixed together on the wings.
Customer note: "We'll split as evenly as possible — minor variation may occur."
C) All Mixed Together — all 3 sauces tossed on all wings
1. Extra sauce selector (same heat category structure as Step 4)
2. Placement: Tossed on wings | On the side
• Add a side • Add a drink • Add a dessert
Subtext: "We'll do our best — special instructions are not guaranteed."
Always visible. Shows: quantity selector + running total + "Add to Cart"
"Add to Cart" disabled until Steps 1–5 fully complete
Tap while incomplete: auto-scroll to first missing required step, highlight orange
Entry point: "Wing Combo" card on the menu. Follows the same step-by-step structure as the Wings Builder with additional combo-specific steps.
Determines wing weight, number of sides, number of drinks, and total price
Breaded or Non-Breaded (conditional on wing type — Boneless is Non-Breaded only, auto-selected)
Dynamic count based on wing weight. Same flow as Section 5 Step 4.
Only shown if 2+ flavours required. Same logic as Section 5 Step 5.
Customer selects sides. Count determined by combo size.
Customer selects drinks. Count determined by combo size.
Two tabs: Active Orders and Past Orders
CANCELLED (final ❌) — state machine allows from any non-final state
PLACED → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY → OUT_FOR_DELIVERY → DELIVERED (final ✅)
CANCELLED (final ❌) — state machine allows from any non-final state
🔒 CANCELLED: State Machine vs Permission Authority — These Are Different Things
State machine rule: CANCELLED is a valid terminal state from any non-final status.
The API enforces this. Nothing blocks the transition at the DB level.
Permission rule: WHO can trigger CANCELLED depends on role + surface:
• Customer (CUSTOMER_SELF): Only within 2-minute window via their own order page

• KDS employee cancellation request (KDS_CANCEL_REQUEST): At PLACED or ACCEPTED status via Reject button, or any non-final status via chat-cancel flow. Requires mandatory reason. Creates a pending cancellation request; Admin must approve before order moves to CANCELLED.
• KDS employee via chat flow (KDS_CHAT_REQUEST): From any non-final status. Requires mandatory reason. Creates a pending cancellation request; Admin must approve.

• Admin panel direct cancel (ADMIN): From any non-final status. Admin can cancel directly without a pending request. Requires mandatory reason. Admin can also approve/reject pending KDS cancellation requests from this panel.
❌ KITCHEN role: Cannot directly finalize CANCELLED from any surface. KITCHEN may initiate cancellation requests (Admin approval required to move order to CANCELLED).
Devs must enforce the permission rule at the API layer — not just the state machine.
The state machine says CANCELLED is possible. Permissions say who is allowed to do it.
Pickup: waiting for customer. Delivery: waiting for driver.
Customer collected. Increments successful order count.
Not collected. Requires confirmation modal. Increments no-show count.
Failed. Requires confirmation modal. Increments no-show count.
Cancelled. Stored in history with cancelled_by + reason.
🔒 Once a final status is reached: all further status changes rejected by API (400/409). KDS and Admin UI disable all action buttons.
Order number, date and time, item summary, order total, current status
"Reorder" button on past orders — see reorder behaviour below
Clicking Reorder attempts to repopulate the cart with the same items from the past order.
The system must validate each item at current state before adding, not blindly clone the snapshot.
→ Add to cart at current price (NOT snapshot price — snapshots are for historical records, not reorders)
→ Skip the item. Show banner: "Some items are currently unavailable and were not added to your cart: [item name(s)]"
→ Skip the item. Include in the unavailable banner above.
Item modifiers changed (group or options removed/added):
→ Add item with still-valid modifiers applied. Removed modifiers are included in the revalidation diff shown to the customer (never dropped silently).
→ If required modifier group no longer exists: skip the item and include in the banner.

→ After cart is populated, show a structured reorder diff listing: items skipped (with reason), modifiers dropped, price changes, and required modifier groups missing. Customer must review and confirm or abandon the cart before proceeding to checkout.
→ Add at current price. No special notice needed — price is always shown before checkout.
If nothing could be added (all items unavailable):
→ Show: "We're sorry, none of the items from this order are currently available."
→ Do not open an empty cart or navigate away.

KDS and Manager Operations belong to one shared operational platform governed by the same permission matrix. The logged-in user’s employee_profiles.role determines which actions are visible and executable. All KDS employees (KITCHEN or MANAGER role): accept, set preparing, set ready, no-show confirmation, ETA adjust, reject/request cancellation with mandatory reason, initiate refund requests, assign drivers, approve busy-driver override, start/complete delivery, and enter delivery PIN. KDS cancellation and refund initiation do not execute directly — they create requests that Admin must approve. Admin Panel: approve/reject cancellation requests, approve/issue refunds, direct cancel of any non-final order, catalog, reporting, settings, support tickets, inventory, employees, drivers, credit issuance, and audits. Every workflow sentence in this PRD that says “KDS employee” means any authenticated KDS user (KITCHEN or MANAGER). The two-tier permission model is: KDS employees handle all operational execution including delivery; Admin handles financial/high-authority approvals (cancellation finalization, refund issuance). For launch, the operational domain may be presented as one interface with role-based controls or as two route groups/workspaces (KDS Workspace for ticket-line execution, prep flow, and ready states; Manager Operations Workspace for exception handling, dispatch, reassignment, overrides, and operational review), provided capabilities remain consistent with the canonical permission model. Manager-specific exception, dispatch, and override workflows may live in a separate Manager Operations workspace without creating a separate authorization model. These

are not separate auth systems and not separate role models — they are two operational surfaces inside the same ops platform. Admin Panel = a separate admin-facing management UI outside of the ops platform, used for catalog, settings, reporting, support tickets, inventory, employee management, cancellation approval, refund approval/issuance, and exceptional overrides. Chat-based cancellation requests are available to all KDS employees from the KDS card controls.
TOP: Order type badge (PICKUP — green | DELIVERY — orange
Order number + scheduled time (pickup time or delivery time)
Customer stats: Total Orders: X | No-Shows: Y
"Prepayment Required" badge if customer flag is set
Item list with quantities, flavours, saucing method, modifiers, removed ingredients, add-ons
ETA display with 10-second adjustment window on new order arrival (see Section 11)
Action buttons (changes based on current status — see table below)
Print controls: Print Ticket button (triggers local print agent)
Assigned printer shown by station (configurable per device — see Section 24)
⚠️ KDS Cancel Policy — Design Decision (aligned with Section 11 review)
Cancel is NOT exposed as a button on the KDS for operational status updates.

Cancellation from KDS follows an initiate-then-approve workflow. Any KDS employee may request cancellation with mandatory reason capture and full audit logging. The order remains in its current operational status until Admin approves the cancellation request. Admin approval moves the order to CANCELLED. Admin rejection closes the request and the order continues normally. Admin can also directly cancel any non-final order from the Admin panel without a request.

All KDS employees (role: KITCHEN or MANAGER) can: Accept, Set Preparing, Set Ready, No-Show, Adjust ETA, Request Cancellation with mandatory reason (Admin approval required), Initiate Refund Request (Admin approval required).

Any KDS employee (role: KITCHEN or MANAGER) can request rejection of a new or accepted order with a mandatory reason (see 7.5.1). The rejection request is sent to Admin for approval. The order remains active until Admin approves.

KDS cancellation request is available at PLACED and ACCEPTED status via the Reject button, and at any non-final status via the chat-cancel flow. All KDS-initiated cancellation creates a pending request; only Admin can approve and finalize to CANCELLED.
Full cancellation after PREPARING or later is available via KDS chat-cancel request flow (any KDS employee, mandatory reason, Admin approval required) or directly from the Admin panel.
Why: Direct KDS final cancellation is not allowed because it creates an accountability gap.
KDS employees may submit cancellation requests with mandatory reason and audit logging. Admin approval is required for final cancellation.
Mandatory-reason request at PLACED/ACCEPTED keeps operations clean. The request-then-approve model ensures accountability without blocking operational speed.
Accept • Request Cancel with mandatory reason (pending Admin approval)
Set Preparing • Adjust ETA • Request Cancel with mandatory reason (pending Admin approval)
Set Ready • Adjust ETA • Request Cancel via chat flow (pending Admin approval)
Mark Picked Up • Mark No-Show (confirm modal) • Adjust ETA
Assign Driver • Adjust ETA • Approve Busy-Driver Override • Start Delivery (after assignment)
Mark Delivered (requires valid 4-digit PIN entry) • Mark No-Show Delivery (confirm modal)
🚫 KDS Reject Flow — KDS Employee Initiated, Admin Approved
Scenario: order arrives but cannot be fulfilled (out of stock, kitchen closing early, etc.).
1️⃣ KDS employee clicks "Request Cancel" on the KDS card
2️⃣ A modal appears requiring a mandatory reason (free text, min 5 characters)
Examples: "Out of stock", "Kitchen closing early", "Item no longer available"
3️⃣ KDS employee confirms → cancellation_request record created (status = PENDING). Order remains in current status. KDS card shows "Cancel Requested" badge. Admin notified.
• requested_by_user_id = KDS employee who initiated the request
On Admin approval, the order record is updated with:
• cancelled_by_user_id = Admin who approved the cancellation
• cancellation_reason = carried from the cancellation request
• cancellation_source = KDS_CANCEL_REQUEST (set when Admin approves the request)
5️⃣ Customer is notified of cancellation with the reason
6️⃣ Admin can see all KDS-rejected orders in Cancelled Orders section
Filtered view: "Cancellation Requests" shows requesting employee name + role, Admin approver name, reason, request time, approval time, and order details
This prevents staff rejecting orders because they don't feel like working — full accountability.
Triggered by clicking "Mark No-Show" when pickup order is in READY status.
Modal: "Are you sure the customer did not come to pick up this order?"
Buttons: [ Yes, mark as No-Show ] [ No, go back ]
On Yes: → Status → NO_SHOW_PICKUP → finalization event + audit entry → no-show count
On No: modal closes, zero DB changes.
Triggered by clicking "Mark No-Show Delivery" when order is in OUT_FOR_DELIVERY.
Modal: "Are you sure the delivery was not completed?"
Buttons: [ Yes, mark as No-Show ] [ No, go back ]
On Yes: same audit + counter logic as pickup. Driver is_on_delivery reset to false.
KDS Customer Stats Display (under customer name on every order card)
[ Prepayment Required ] ← prominent badge when prepayment_required = true
When total_no_shows >= 3: system sets prepayment_required = true
Admin can override without deleting history
No-show count is never decremented — history is immutable
At checkout if prepayment_required = true: "pay later" options blocked. Message: "Prepayment required due to past no-shows."
Driver assignment is managed from the KDS in MVP. A dedicated driver app is a future milestone. All existing statuses and workflows from Sections 7.2–7.5 remain valid.
Admin can create, edit, activate, deactivate, and view driver profiles from the admin panel. Inactive drivers never appear in the KDS assignment list.
Unique at the identity layer via user_identities (see Section 10.0).
driver_profiles.phone_number mirrors the canonical phone from user_identities for operational convenience.
Mirrors email from user_identities for operational convenience; canonical uniqueness enforced at identity layer
• Admin can manually set availability_status to AVAILABLE, UNAVAILABLE, or OFF_SHIFT from the driver profile page
• Admin CANNOT manually set ON_DELIVERY — that state is system-derived only (set atomically when Start Delivery is clicked)
• Example: marking a driver UNAVAILABLE due to vehicle issue, without requiring clock-out
• When driver app launches, driver can set themselves AVAILABLE or UNAVAILABLE from their device
• Timeclock remains the authoritative source for shift tracking; driver self-toggle is supplementary
1️⃣ Delivery order reaches READY → "Assign to Driver" action appears on KDS card
2️⃣ Clicking opens a searchable driver picker
3️⃣ Selecting a driver → confirmation modal (available driver) or override modal (busy driver)
4️⃣ On confirm: assigned_driver_user_id set, assigned_driver_at recorded, audit event written
5️⃣ KDS shows an Estimated Travel Time input:
• KDS employee enters estimated travel time in minutes (e.g. 20)
• Stored as estimated_travel_minutes on the order — NOT as estimated_arrival_at yet
• estimated_arrival_at is computed when Start Delivery is clicked (see below)
• Customer UI shows: "Expected arrival: approx. 20 minutes after departure"
6️⃣ KDS card shows: assigned driver name + entered travel time estimate
7️⃣ Customer order detail page updates in real time with driver name + phone + travel estimate
Assignment step: stores estimated_travel_minutes (integer, minutes entered by KDS employee)
Start Delivery click: delivery_started_at recorded + estimated_arrival_at = delivery_started_at + estimated_travel_minutes
This prevents the formula depending on a timestamp that does not exist yet.
Future driver app: driver can update estimated_travel_minutes from their device; estimated_arrival_at recomputes.
After driver is assigned, customer order detail page shows:
• Estimated arrival time window (e.g. "Expected by 7:15 – 7:25 PM")
• No other driver details shown to customer in MVP
READY (after assignment): "Driver assigned — preparing to depart"
OUT_FOR_DELIVERY (overdue): "Your driver is almost there — hang tight! 😊" (see 7.8.8)
7.8.5 Delivery Start & Completion (MVP — No Driver App)
After assignment + ETA entry, KDS shows "Start Delivery" button.
On click: → status → OUT_FOR_DELIVERY → delivery_started_at recorded → estimated_arrival_at = delivery_started_at + estimated_travel_minutes → audit event → customer UI updates
Driver state: is_on_delivery = true AND availability_status = ON_DELIVERY (both set atomically)
Complete Delivery — with Customer PIN Verification
Driver calls store when trip is done. KDS employee marks DELIVERED in KDS after entering the delivery PIN.
Before marking DELIVERED, the system requires a 4-digit delivery confirmation PIN.
1️⃣ When order is dispatched (OUT_FOR_DELIVERY), a 4-digit PIN is generated and sent to the customer
2️⃣ Customer shows this PIN to the driver at the door
3️⃣ Driver calls store and relays the PIN to the KDS employee on duty
4️⃣ KDS employee enters PIN on KDS before clicking "Mark Delivered"
5️⃣ If PIN matches: → status → DELIVERED → delivered_at recorded → driver state reset (see below) → audit event → customer UI updates
6️⃣ If PIN does not match: KDS shows error "Incorrect delivery PIN."
Each individual failure also logged as a separate admin_audit_logs row (action = PIN_FAIL, order_id, timestamp).
Retry limits: max 5 failed attempts before PIN entry is locked for this order.
On lockout: KDS shows "PIN entry locked. Use KDS override to complete delivery."
7️⃣ PIN bypass: KDS employee can override with mandatory reason (logged in audit)
KDS employee override available even if PIN is locked; bypass_reason required.

PIN expiry is checked at KDS entry time (compare NOW() against delivery_pin_verifications.expires_at). If the PIN has expired: KDS shows “Delivery PIN has expired.” with two options: (1) Regenerate PIN — system generates a new 4-digit PIN, sends it to the customer, resets expires_at and failed_attempts. Driver must obtain the new PIN from the customer. (2) KDS employee bypass — same flow as PIN mismatch bypass, with mandatory reason logged. A new PIN is NOT automatically sent on expiry — regeneration requires explicit KDS employee action. The customer’s original PIN simply stops working after delivery_pin_expiry_minutes (default 240 min).
Note: When driver app is built, driver enters PIN directly in the app — no phone call needed.
On confirm: → status → NO_SHOW_DELIVERY → delivery_no_show_at recorded → driver state reset (see below) → no-show confirmation flow enforced → customer no-show count +1
📌 Canonical Driver State Reset Rule (DELIVERED, NO_SHOW_DELIVERY, or Unassignment):
availability_status = AVAILABLE (if driver is still clocked in and on shift)
availability_status = OFF_SHIFT (if driver has since clocked out)
Both fields must be updated atomically in the same DB transaction.
Never update one without the other. State drift between is_on_delivery and availability_status
will cause the KDS driver picker to show incorrect availability.

(Future — Driver App): Driver receives push notification on assignment, sets their own ETA, enters customer PIN directly, updates delivery status from their device. Changes propagate to Customer UI + KDS + Admin within 3 seconds.
Reassignment allowed while status is READY or OUT_FOR_DELIVERY (admin override only for the later)
Reassignment not allowed once order reaches a final status
On reassignment: old driver removed from order, new assigned_driver_user_id + assigned_driver_at written, audit log records both old and new driver
Old driver's is_on_delivery flag recalculated based on any remaining live assignments
Admin can open any driver profile and view their full delivery history.
Assigned at / Start time / Completion time
Driver detail summary (top of driver profile):
Current status: Available / On Delivery / Off Shift
Trigger: estimated_arrival_at has passed but order status is still OUT_FOR_DELIVERY.
• Status label changes from "Out for delivery 🚴" to:
"Your driver is almost there — hang tight! 😊"
• A gentle reassurance message prevents premature customer complaints
• No alarm or aggressive messaging — keep the tone calm and positive
• System automatically creates a support ticket (see Section 25):
• Admin sees the ticket in support panel
• Customer is NOT notified of the auto-ticket — it is an internal alert only

• Deduplication rule (non-negotiable): at most ONE auto-generated overdue-delivery ticket per order. Before creating, the background job must check for an existing OPEN, IN_REVIEW, WAITING_ON_CUSTOMER, or RESOLVED ticket where order_id matches and ticket_type = DELIVERY_ISSUE and description starts with “Auto-generated”. If one exists, skip creation. This prevents the 60-second job from spamming duplicate tickets every cycle.
• If delivery completes before admin reviews: ticket auto-resolves
event_type = STATUS_CHANGE, from_value = OPEN or IN_REVIEW, to_value = RESOLVED
performed_by = NULL, note = “Auto-resolved: delivery completed before admin review”
note = "Auto-resolved: delivery completed before admin review"
support_ticket_events row written with note = the same message (resolution notes belong on support_ticket_resolutions or support_ticket_events, not as a separate column on support_tickets)
• Admin can create, edit, activate, deactivate driver records
• Driver list is searchable by partial name match
• Only active, on-shift drivers appear in KDS assignment picker
• Busy drivers display "Already on delivery" badge and require override confirmation
• Assign Driver button appears only for READY delivery orders
• KDS employee enters estimated travel time in minutes (stored as estimated_travel_minutes; NOT computed as arrival time until Start Delivery is clicked)
• estimated_arrival_at = delivery_started_at + estimated_travel_minutes (computed on Start Delivery click only)
• Schema supports future GPS-based ETA updates from driver app
• 4-digit PIN generated when order moves to OUT_FOR_DELIVERY; sent to customer
• KDS employee enters PIN on KDS before marking DELIVERED
• PIN mismatch: KDS shows error; delivery not marked complete
• KDS employee can bypass PIN with mandatory reason (logged in audit)
• Customer sees "almost there" message when estimated_arrival_at passes
• Auto support ticket created when estimated_arrival_at + grace period passes
• Auto-ticket resolves if delivery completes before admin reviews

• ⚠️ Operational prerequisite: overdue auto-ticketing is a monitored automation, not magic. Owner and KDS employees must tune ETA entry discipline and the overdue_delivery_grace_minutes setting before relying on this feature. If ETA estimates are sloppy or KDS employees skip Start Delivery status updates, the system will create false-alarm tickets for ordinary late-but-fine deliveries. Review auto-ticket volume weekly during the first month and adjust the grace period accordingly.
WebSockets for live status, ETA, and overdue delivery changes
Status changes reach customer UI within 3 seconds of server event
Overdue delivery detection runs on a scheduled background job (every 60 seconds check)
When determining if an item can be added to a cart or purchased at checkout, evaluate in this order:
1️⃣ deleted_at is set (soft-deleted) → NOT purchasable. Never shown to customer.
2️⃣ is_active = false → NOT purchasable. Not shown in menu.
3️⃣ is_available = false (sold out) → NOT purchasable. Shown greyed out with "Sold Out" badge.
4️⃣ Outside menu_item_schedules window → NOT purchasable. Shown greyed with availability hours.
5️⃣ Not valid for current fulfillment_type → NOT purchasable for this mode. Hidden or greyed.
Backend must enforce this precedence at checkout, not just at menu display time.
Frontend display is convenience; backend revalidation is enforcement.
🛝 General Checkout Cart Revalidation — Required at Every Checkout Attempt
At checkout (before order creation), the backend must revalidate ALL of the following:
• Item availability — using the precedence rules above
• Modifier validity — required groups still exist; selected options still active
• Flavour validity — selected wing flavours still active; saucing method still valid for count
• Promo eligibility — code still valid, not expired, within usage limits, fulfillment type matches
• Scheduling — selected time slot still within store hours and lead time
• Prepayment gate — if customer has prepayment_required = true, pay-later blocked
• Delivery zone — address postal code still in allowed_postal_codes
• Minimum subtotal — meets delivery minimum if applicable
This is NOT the same as the mode-change revalidation in Section 2.3 (which is mid-session).
This is the final server-side guard at checkout. It catches catalog drift, promo expiry,
and any state change that happened between cart build and order submission.
If any check fails: return a clear error per failed item/promo. Never silently drop items.
Customer must see what was removed and why before submitting the order.
Cannot proceed to menu or checkout without a valid delivery address
Time selector only shows slots within operating hours. No manual time entry.
Time options before the 30-minute cutoff are disabled or hidden
Add to Cart blocked; auto-scroll to first incomplete section on tap
Slots dynamically shown/hidden. Cannot add more or fewer than required.
Always in heat category list. Never hidden or greyed out.
Step 5 required when 2 or 3 flavours selected. Sub-question required if "sauce on side" chosen.
Card greyed out with "Currently Unavailable". Tapping does nothing.

Idempotency key sent with checkout. Backend deduplicates. Checkout idempotency must cover all side effects: wallet credit deduction (CREDIT_USED ledger entry), promo usage_count increment, and payment authorization must all be wrapped in a single transaction. If any step fails, the entire checkout rolls back — no partial wallet deductions, no orphaned promo usage increments. Wallet application must use SELECT FOR UPDATE or equivalent row-level locking to prevent concurrent checkouts from double-spending the same credit balance. Promo usage_count increment must be atomic and checked within the same transaction as the usage limit comparison.
Cart items preserved. Warning banner shown.
Phone number + OTP required. No full signup required.
If prepayment_required = true: pay-later options blocked at checkout.
API rejects any status change on finalized order. UI disables all buttons.
Assign Driver button appears only for READY delivery orders.
Selecting a busy driver shows confirmation modal before assignment.
Tip selector appears only on delivery order checkout.
Customer can rate driver only for completed DELIVERED delivery orders.
Unique constraint: one driver_delivery_review per order_id.
Only one promo code active per order unless explicitly enabled by admin.
Store credit reduces payable amount but cannot go below $0.
Admin-marked sold-out items shown with badge. Cannot be added to cart.
No price change applied online. Checkbox at checkout: "I am a student — I will present my student ID at pickup."
This note is printed on the kitchen receipt. Cashier verifies ID and applies discount in person.
student_discount_requested boolean default false -- customer checked the box at checkout
student_discount_verified_by FK users(id) nullable -- cashier who verified ID and applied discount
student_discount_amount_cents int default 0 -- discount amount applied by cashier at counter
Reporting: filter orders where student_discount_requested = true to count student discount events.
Admin can see which cashier verified each student discount via student_discount_verified_by.

Items below are not in MVP scope. Most are designed to layer on cleanly. Some may require new tables or fields but should not break existing core flows. "No schema changes required" was too sweeping a promise — the honest version is: additive where possible, isolated new tables where necessary.
Real-time driver GPS on customer order page
Driver receives assignments + updates status from device
Scheduled orders — already in MVP via date selectors
✅ Already built. Sections 2.1 and 2.2 include date + time selectors. This is not a future feature.
✅ Already in Section 2.2. Hand to me / Leave at door / Call on arrival are part of the delivery flow at launch.
Menu item scheduling — already in MVP
✅ Required at launch for Lunch Specials (11 AM–3 PM daily). Schema defined in Section 10.31. Must be built at launch, not later.
Address book with Home / Work / Other labels for repeat customers
Block orders outside defined postal codes or delivery zone
Different fees per delivery zone or postal code
Block small delivery orders below a set threshold
Store-credit refunds are MVP and active now. Refund to original payment method (Stripe/gateway) is schema-ready and UI-visible but disabled until payment gateway is integrated. See Section 12.6.
Handed to customer / Left at door / Unreachable flag
Estimated delivery window — already in MVP
✅ Already in Section 7.8.3–7.8.4. Manager enters estimated_travel_minutes; customer sees "Expected arrival" window. estimated_arrival_at drives overdue detection. This is not a future feature.
Order issue reporting — already in MVP

✅ Already in Section 25. Customer issue flow, ticket creation, admin handling, and ACs are all defined. The customer-facing report button on order detail page is also part of MVP scope — not near-term.
Points balance + redemption at checkout (schema ready)
Order confirmed, ready, out for delivery, delivered, cancelled
Customer saves items for quick reorder
Spicy level, halal, vegetarian, dairy, nuts flags per item
Suggest fries, dip, drink, dessert when cart has wings
Bundled price for wing + fries + drink + dip
Homepage banner + promo item with start/end date and countdown
Count no-shows in last 90 days only (vs all-time)
MVP source of truth is timeclock (clock-in = AVAILABLE, clock-out = OFF_SHIFT). Admin override allowed now. Driver self-toggle from their own app is a future feature.
Total deliveries, avg rating, no-show count, avg delivery time, tips
✅ Already specified in Section 3.4. Real-time name/description filtering is part of the launch menu page. Not a future feature.

Reference for the backend team. All tables, columns, and constraints arising from the feature set defined in this PRD. This version includes corrections from schema review: fulfillment_type/order_source separation, relational promo targets, full identity model, payment transactions, order status events, and admin audit logs.

One users table is the single source of truth for every person in the system. Role-specific data lives in profile extension tables, not in users itself. This prevents auth and reporting from becoming a mess as the system grows.
users ← root identity for every person (customer, staff, admin, driver)
├─ customer_profiles ← extends users for customers (loyalty counters, credit balance)
├─ employee_profiles ← extends users for staff (role, PIN, hourly rate, hire date)
└─ driver_profiles ← extends employee_profiles for drivers (vehicle, availability, delivery stats)
user_identities ← login methods per user (PHONE OTP, GOOGLE OAuth)
auth_otp_codes ← OTP management (or delegated to Firebase / Supabase Auth)
-- STAFF is a broad actor type for all restaurant employees.
-- Authorization granularity (MANAGER | CASHIER | KITCHEN) lives in employee_profiles.role.
-- See Section 10.0.1 for the canonical auth rule.
🔑 Canonical Auth Rule — Two Layers, One Source of Truth Per Layer
Layer 1: users.role — coarse actor type for routing and basic access
• STAFF → any restaurant employee (all staff roles map here at the JWT layer)

• ADMIN → full admin panel access: approve/reject cancellation requests, approve/issue refunds, direct cancel, catalog, reporting, settings, support tickets, inventory, employees, drivers, credit issuance, audits
Layer 2: employee_profiles.role — fine-grained staff authorization (STAFF users only)

For KDS permissions, Section 7.5 is canonical. Section 10.0 summarizes identity/auth layering only and must not narrow or override Section 7.5. If any wording below conflicts with the KDS role model in Section 7.5, Section 7.5 wins.
• MANAGER → all KITCHEN actions (same full KDS operational set), plus approve drawer, edit punches, override prepayment flag. Cannot approve cancellation requests or issue refunds (Admin only).
• CASHIER → create walk-in orders, apply coupons, clock in/out

• KITCHEN → full KDS operational actions: accept, reject/request cancel, set preparing, set ready, no-show, ETA adjust, assign driver, approve busy-driver override, start/complete delivery, enter delivery PIN, initiate refund request. Cannot approve cancellation or issue refunds (Admin only).
• DRIVER → drivers who are also employed staff; same as CASHIER for POS/timeclock access
⚠️ ADMIN is NOT in employee_profiles.role. Admin staff use users.role = ADMIN at the JWT layer.
⚠️ DRIVER is NOT duplicated: it only appears in employee_profiles when a person is both a driver AND a cashier/kitchen worker.
The single role field cannot cleanly represent "this person is both a driver and a cashier".
For MVP: employee_profiles.role = DRIVER grants CASHIER-equivalent POS/timeclock access.
This works because the permission sets do not conflict for MVP scope.
Long-term limitation: if DRIVER and CASHIER ever need distinct permissions on the same surface,

a role-assignments model (user has many roles) would be needed instead of one role field. employee_role_assignments (schema defined — build when first hybrid-role request arrives) employee_user_id FK employee_profiles(user_id) role ENUM: MANAGER | CASHIER | KITCHEN | DRIVER location_id FK locations(id) is_primary boolean default true PK: (employee_user_id, role, location_id) -- Replaces the single employee_profiles.role field with a many-to-many model. -- Migration path: copy existing employee_profiles.role into one row per employee, -- then drop employee_profiles.role column and read from this table instead. -- All middleware role checks must be updated to query this table. -- Do NOT build this now. Build it the moment a second hybrid role is requested.

That is a future architecture decision, not an MVP blocker. Do not extend this workaround by adding more hybrid meanings to employee_profiles.role. If a new combination arises (e.g. driver + kitchen), migrate to a role-assignments model first. Mark this as a migration trigger point in the system blueprint: the moment any new hybrid role is requested, stop and build the many-roles model instead of mutating this enum further.
🗓️ Endpoint category → which role source governs it:
If STAFF: employee_profiles.role = KITCHEN | MANAGER. If ADMIN: bypass employee_profiles check — admin has full KDS access.

KDS employee actions (all roles: all operational actions including delivery controls, request cancellation with reason, initiate refund request — cancellation and refund requests require Admin approval). Admin-only actions (approve/reject cancellation requests, approve/issue refund, direct cancel from Admin panel). This row is illustrative — the canonical permission set is defined in Section 7.0 KDS role definition.

If STAFF: any employee_profiles.role (KITCHEN or MANAGER) for all operational KDS actions including delivery controls, cancellation requests, and refund-initiation. Cancellation and refund requests require Admin approval. If ADMIN: bypass — can approve requests, cancel directly, and issue refunds.
Never check employee_profiles for CUSTOMER users
🔑 ADMIN Bypass Rule for KDS and Staff Surfaces
When users.role = ADMIN, skip the employee_profiles.role check entirely.
ADMIN is always permitted on any staff-accessible endpoint, including KDS.
This avoids the contradiction where "Admin has full access" but KDS checks employee_profiles.
if (user.role === ADMIN) { allow; return; } // bypass staff role check
// else: check employee_profiles.role for STAFF users

ADMIN users do not need an employee_profiles row. The users.role = ADMIN is sufficient. ADMIN bypasses employee_profiles.role checks only — it does not bypass location scoping (admin_location_assignments), object ownership checks, or workflow-state rules (e.g. final-status lock, cancel permissions). Every admin action is still scoped to their assigned location(s) and still obeys

state-machine constraints. To be explicit: “if (user.role === ADMIN) { allow }” applies ONLY to the employee_profiles.role gate. It must NOT be copy-pasted into location-scoping middleware, state-machine validators, or object-ownership checks. Those are separate enforcement layers that ADMIN must still pass. Implementation rule: the ADMIN bypass must live in exactly one middleware — the staff-role gate. Location middleware, state-machine middleware, and ownership middleware must never contain an ADMIN shortcut. If a code review finds “if ADMIN, skip” in any middleware other than the staff-role gate, that is a bug, not a feature.
provider_user_id text -- E.164 phone or Google sub
Decision: Guest checkout creates a users row immediately on order placement.
• Every order must have a traceable identity for no-show tracking, prepayment gate, and support tickets.
• A temp guest identity that is never converted creates orphaned records and reporting gaps.
• Guest provides phone + OTP at checkout (same as registered user phone login).
• System checks if a users row exists for that phone via user_identities.
• If yes: use the existing user_id. If no: create users row + user_identities row immediately.
• Guest is offered account creation (password / Google link) after order confirmation.
• All orders placed with the same phone number are attributed to the same user_id.
• No-show counters and prepayment flags apply to the user_id, not just the snapshot phone.

Every guest order creates a real users row + user_identities row immediately — there are no temporary, partial, or ghost accounts anywhere in the system. OTP verification at checkout establishes identity trust (is_verified = true on user_identities). Password remains nullable on users until the guest explicitly completes full signup (password set or Google OAuth linked). Role is CUSTOMER from the moment the users row is created — it does not change on signup. If a returning guest places another order with the same phone, the existing user_id is reused — never create a second users row for the same verified phone. After order confirmation, guest is prompted to complete signup (set password / link Google). Declining does not delete or downgrade the account — the user_id persists with full order history, no-show counters, and credit wallet intact.
Phone number is the MVP identity key. This is practical for a restaurant context but has known edge cases:
• Shared family phones: two people in the same household share one number. Their order history,
no-show counts, prepayment flags, and credit wallets will merge under one user_id.
• Reused numbers: phone numbers are recycled by carriers. A new user with a previously
used number inherits the history of the original holder.
• Business / shared household phones: same merge problem as shared family.
• These are accepted trade-offs for MVP. Phone-only identity is simple and frictionless.
• Admin can manually override prepayment flags, clear credits, or split accounts on a case-by-case basis.
• The system does NOT magically resolve shared phones. It relies on admin discretion for edge cases.
Displayed as-is in UI and on receipts
Dedicated enum. Not MILD, not NULL.
"PLAIN" must be a named enum value, not NULL.
HALF_HALF, MIXED, SIDE, SPLIT_EVENLY, TWO_MIXED_ONE_SIDE, ALL_MIXED
Populated when method = SIDE or TWO_MIXED_ONE_SIDE
customer_profiles extends users(id) for customer-specific data. Every customer has one row in users and one row here. Do not store auth credentials or login methods here.
user_id FK users(id) PK -- same id as users row
total_successful_orders int default 0 -- CACHE: increment on PICKED_UP or DELIVERED
total_no_shows int default 0 -- CACHE: increment on NO_SHOW_PICKUP or NO_SHOW_DELIVERY
-- It is a cached accumulator, NOT the source of truth for windowed queries.
-- Future "last 90 days" logic must query order_finalization_events
-- or order_status_events directly, not this counter.
-- store_credit_cents REMOVED: credit balance lives only in customer_wallets.credit_balance_cents
⚠️ total_no_shows is a cache, not a source of truth
current_no_shows is an all-time accumulating counter. It is immutable — never decremented.
It is correct and useful for the current MVP prepayment gate (3 strikes, any time).
When the backlog item "Windowed no-show counts (last 90 days)" is implemented:
• Do NOT try to adjust or reset total_no_shows.
• The event table is the source of truth. The counter is a fast lookup cache.
Two fields were conflated in previous drafts. They are now separated cleanly.
fulfillment_type vs order_source — Why These Are Different
fulfillment_type describes HOW the order is fulfilled (PICKUP or DELIVERY). Always exactly one of these.
order_source describes WHERE the order originated (which channel / surface created it).
The old order_type included IN_STORE and PHONE which are sales channels, not fulfillment methods.
This created overlap: a phone order for pickup would set order_type = PHONE and source = PHONE simultaneously, which is nonsense.

In UI copy (customer-facing pages, KDS display, admin dashboards), “Order type” may be displayed to staff and customers. In specifications, APIs, DTOs, analytics labels, admin filters, DB columns, and all code, always use fulfillment_type. This rule applies equally to KDS column headers, cancelled-order report columns, and admin filter dropdowns. Enforce in code review.
How is the order fulfilled? Always required.
Examples showing both fields used together:
Customer orders on website for pickup
Customer orders on website for delivery
Employee enters a phoned-in delivery order
Admin creates order from admin panel
location_id FK locations(id) -- REQUIRED: every order belongs to a location; scopes all KDS, admin, and reporting queries
customer_user_id FK users(id) nullable -- null for walk-in orders without a known customer
current_status order_status_enum NOT NULL default PLACED
-- This field + order_status_events together = full status picture.
fulfillment_type ENUM: PICKUP | DELIVERY -- HOW it is fulfilled
order_source ENUM: ONLINE | POS | PHONE | ADMIN -- WHERE it came from

requires_manual_review boolean default false -- true when KDS was offline at auto-accept timeout. Clearing rules: set to false automatically when order is manually ACCEPTED (any role). Also set to false when a KDS cancellation request is approved by Admin. Alert banner in admin/KDS is driven by this flag, not by UI dismissal.
-- Delivery address snapshot (define shape early; agree across frontend and backend)
delivery_address_snapshot JSONB nullable -- { line1, line2, city, postal, buzzer, instructions }

cancellation_source ENUM: CUSTOMER_SELF | KDS_CANCEL_REQUEST | KDS_CHAT_REQUEST | ADMIN | SYSTEM nullable -- KDS_CANCEL_REQUEST and KDS_CHAT_REQUEST are set only after Admin approves the pending request. ADMIN = direct admin cancel. CUSTOMER_SELF = self-cancel within window.
cancellation_chat_conversation_id FK order_conversations(id) nullable -- set when cancellation initiated via live order chat
estimated_travel_minutes int nullable -- entered by KDS employee at assignment step
estimated_arrival_at timestamptz nullable -- computed = delivery_started_at + estimated_travel_minutes (on Start Delivery click)
payment_method ENUM: CASH | CARD | STORE_CREDIT | SPLIT | WAIVED nullable -- SUMMARY FIELD ONLY. Source of truth = order_payments rows. SPLIT means multiple rows with different methods.

payment_status_summary ENUM (order_payment_status_summary): UNPAID | PENDING | PARTIALLY_PAID | PAID | PARTIALLY_REFUNDED | REFUNDED | FAILED | VOIDED -- SUMMARY FIELD ONLY. Derived from order_payments rows. See §10.5E canonical rule.
created_by_user_id FK users(id) nullable -- for POS and PHONE orders

The current status column on orders tells you where an order is now. order_status_events tells you every transition: who changed it, from what, to what, and when. This is separate from order_events (which handles general notes and ETA changes) and distinct from order_finalization_events (which handles no-show confirmations).
from_status order_status_enum nullable -- null on first transition (PLACED); use same enum as orders.current_status
to_status order_status_enum -- the new status; same canonical enum; typos = build error not runtime surprise
performed_by_user_id FK users(id) nullable -- null for system-triggered transitions

source ENUM: CUSTOMER | STAFF | ADMIN | SYSTEM | DRIVER -- coarse actor type for generic audit timeline. DRIVER here means a STAFF user with employee_profiles.role = DRIVER acting in driver context (not a separate user_role).
action_source text nullable -- granular action identifier for analytics:
-- source is for who; action_source is for how
-- Index: (order_id, created_at DESC) for fast timeline queries
-- PLACED | ACCEPTED | PREPARING | READY | OUT_FOR_DELIVERY | PICKED_UP |
-- This same enum type must be used on:
-- Keeping them in sync via a shared DB type prevents silent drift.
• The current status field tells you WHERE the order is. This table tells you the full journey.
• Required for: KDS audit trail, dispute resolution, delivery SLA analysis, customer timeline display.
• order_finalization_events handles no-show confirmation modals specifically.
• This table handles all status transitions generically and replaces the need to scatter event writes across multiple tables.
Complete menu schema for reference. These tables power the ordering UI, the wings builder, and modifier flows.
price_cents int -- ALWAYS integers, never floats
image_url text nullable -- S3/CDN URL only; never served from app server
deleted_at timestamptz nullable -- soft delete; never hard delete
price_delta_cents int default 0 -- can be 0 or positive

Snapshots are the most important pattern in the schema. Every order item stores the name and price at the time of ordering. This means historical orders remain accurate even after menu changes, price updates, or item deletions.
menu_item_id FK menu_items(id) nullable -- nullable: item may be deleted later
name_snapshot text -- frozen name at order time
unit_price_cents_snapshot int -- frozen price at order time
saucing_method ENUM: HALF_HALF | MIXED | SIDE | SPLIT_EVENLY | TWO_MIXED_ONE_SIDE | ALL_MIXED nullable
price_delta_cents_snapshot int -- frozen modifier price delta
delivery_address_snapshot on orders is a JSONB field. Define and agree on this shape early across frontend and backend:
Why snapshot instead of FK to customer_addresses?
• Customer may delete or edit their address book after placing.
• The delivery was made to a specific address at a specific time. That must never change.

orders.payment_method is a summary field for reporting/UI only. orders.payment_status_summary is a summary field for reporting/UI only (enum: order_payment_status_summary — UNPAID, PENDING, PARTIALLY_PAID, PAID, PARTIALLY_REFUNDED, REFUNDED, FAILED, VOIDED). The canonical source of truth for payment lifecycle is order_payments (append-only transaction rows). Each row represents one money event using payment_tender_method (CASH, CARD, STORE_CREDIT, WAIVED — never SPLIT), transaction_type (AUTH, CAPTURE, VOID, REFUND, ADJUSTMENT), and transaction_status (PENDING, SUCCESS, FAILED, CANCELLED). This supports auth/capture flows, voids, partial refunds, and split payments without hacking the orders row. Canonical rule: order_payments rows are the source of truth for all money movement. orders.payment_method and orders.payment_status_summary are derived convenience fields for reporting and UI display. If orders.payment_method and order_payments rows ever disagree, the rows win. SPLIT as a payment_method value only appears on orders (meaning this order has multiple order_payments rows with different methods). Split payment is not exposed in MVP POS UX, but the backend transaction model supports it via multiple order_payments rows.
provider text -- STRIPE | SQUARE | CASH | INTERNAL
provider_transaction_id text nullable -- external reference id
transaction_type ENUM (payment_transaction_type): AUTH | CAPTURE | VOID | REFUND | ADJUSTMENT
signed_amount_cents int -- positive for charges, negative for refunds; must be non-zero
transaction_status ENUM (payment_transaction_status): PENDING | SUCCESS | FAILED | CANCELLED
payment_method ENUM (payment_tender_method): CASH | CARD | STORE_CREDIT | WAIVED -- never SPLIT at transaction level; SPLIT is an order-level summary only
1️⃣ AUTH → card pre-authorized at checkout (funds held)
2️⃣ CAPTURE → funds captured when order is ACCEPTED (not READY — capture on ACCEPTED is the policy)
Rationale: ACCEPTED means the kitchen confirmed they will fulfil the order.
Capturing later (READY) creates a larger window where the restaurant has committed but the
customer is not yet charged, complicating void/refund flows if something goes wrong.
3️⃣ REFUND → partial or full refund if admin resolves issue with refund
1️⃣ CAPTURE (CASH) → recorded immediately when customer pays at counter
1️⃣ AUTH → VOID → hold released, customer not charged
2️⃣ CAPTURE (STORE_CREDIT) → remainder from store credit balance
Each portion creates a separate payments row linked to the same order_id.
kds_auto_accept_seconds int default 10 -- configurable auto-accept timeout per location
allowed_postal_codes text[] nullable -- MVP delivery zone: empty = delivery disabled
delivery_fee_cents int default 0 -- flat delivery fee in cents
free_delivery_threshold_cents int nullable -- subtotal above which delivery is free
minimum_delivery_subtotal_cents int default 0 -- minimum subtotal for delivery orders
tax_rate_bps int default 1300 -- basis points: 1300 = 13% HST. Launch default for London, Ontario.
tax_delivery_fee boolean default true -- delivery fees ARE taxable for prepared food in Ontario. Launch default = true.
trusted_ip_ranges jsonb default '[]' -- store IP ranges for POS WiFi restriction
delivery_pin_expiry_minutes int default 240 -- PIN expires after 4 hours
overdue_delivery_grace_minutes int default 20 -- grace before auto support ticket created
manager_credit_limit_cents int default 2000 -- max credit a Manager can issue per action ($20 default)
-- 0 = no limit; must be approved by admin for larger amounts
prepayment_no_show_threshold int default 3 -- no-shows before prepayment_required = true
first_order_discount_enabled boolean default true -- master toggle: when false, all is_first_order_only promos are suppressed at checkout
default_promo_stackable boolean default false -- default stackable value applied to newly created promo codes
support_ticket_autoclose_days int default 14 -- days after RESOLVED before auto-close (0 = never auto-close)
add_items_auto_approve_enabled boolean default false -- if true: change requests auto-approve when order is PLACED (not yet ACCEPTED)
-- if false: all change requests require admin/manager approval regardless of status
final_status ENUM: PICKED_UP | NO_SHOW_PICKUP | DELIVERED | NO_SHOW_DELIVERY | CANCELLED
user_id FK employee_profiles(user_id) PK -- guarantees driver = employee = user
phone_number text UNIQUE -- globally unique; see implementation note below
availability_status ENUM: AVAILABLE | ON_DELIVERY | UNAVAILABLE | OFF_SHIFT
-- Standard UNIQUE constraint = globally unique across all rows, active or not.
-- Rational: a deactivated driver may be reactivated later and should retain their phone.
-- Two different drivers should never share a phone number regardless of active status.
-- The root source of uniqueness is users + user_identities (Section 10.0).
-- driver_profiles.phone_number is an operational convenience copy, not the identity authority.
-- If this becomes a real operational problem (e.g. reassigning a number), admin can
-- clear the old driver's phone_number before creating the new record.
event_type ENUM: ASSIGNED | REASSIGNED | STARTED | DELIVERED | NO_SHOW | UNASSIGNED
order_id FK orders(id) UNIQUE -- one rating per delivery
tag_code text -- e.g. POLITE, LATE, GOOD_COMMUNICATION
sender_surface ENUM: CUSTOMER | KDS | MANAGER | ADMIN

-- read_at column removed. Canonical unread tracking uses chat_side_read_states (order_id, reader_side IN CUSTOMER|STAFF) — see Schema Spec. chat_read_states (order_id, user_id) is optional per-user audit/helper state. PRD §15 defines the behavioral rules.
user_id FK users(id) UNIQUE -- root identity; NOT customers(id) which does not exist
credit_balance_cents int default 0 -- cached, always updated transactionally
-- FK corrected: references users(id), consistent with the root identity model.
-- Both point to the same root user_id.
type ENUM: CREDIT_GRANTED | CREDIT_USED | CREDIT_REVERSED | CREDIT_EXPIRED

Arrays were removed from promo_codes. eligible_product_ids UUID[] and eligible_category_ids UUID[] are replaced with relational join tables. valid_days text[] is replaced with a promo_valid_days table. Relational joins are cleaner, indexable, and do not require array operators.
discount_type ENUM: PERCENTAGE | FIXED_AMOUNT | FREE_DELIVERY | BXGY
discount_value numeric -- type rules by discount_type:
-- PERCENTAGE: decimal percent e.g. 10.0 = 10%. Max 100. No fractions below 0.01.
-- FIXED_AMOUNT: integer cents ONLY e.g. 500 = $5.00. Never floats. Backend must reject non-integer.
-- FREE_DELIVERY: discount_value ignored; delivery fee zeroed.
-- BXGY: discount_value ignored; reward config in promo_bxgy_rules table.
-- ⚠️ Known design debt: one field serves multiple semantic roles depending on discount_type.
-- Workable for MVP. If promo types grow, consider a typed rule payload per type.
eligible_fulfillment_type ENUM: BOTH | PICKUP_ONLY | DELIVERY_ONLY NOT NULL default BOTH
day_of_week ENUM: MON | TUE | WED | THU | FRI | SAT | SUN
status ENUM: NEW | CONTACTED | QUOTED | CLOSED
category text -- Chicken, Sauce, Drinks, Packaging, etc.
unit text -- pcs, kg, g, lb, L, ml, boxes, bags, bottles
adjustment_type ENUM: STOCK_ADDED | STOCK_USED | DAMAGED | CORRECTED | RETURNED
user_id FK users(id) PK -- same id as users row; consistent with customer_profiles pattern
employee_pin_hash text nullable -- bcrypt hash of 5-digit POS login code. Raw code never stored. See API contract: request field is employee_code; storage is employee_pin_hash.
role ENUM: MANAGER | CASHIER | KITCHEN | DRIVER
-- NOTE: ADMIN removed from this enum. Admin staff use users.role = ADMIN at the JWT layer.
-- If an admin also needs fine-grained staff access, give them a separate MANAGER row.
-- DRIVER here means: driver who is also an employed staff member.
-- A driver who is ONLY a driver has no employee_profiles row at all.
-- ⚠️ However: in MVP this person CANNOT be assigned deliveries.
-- They have no employee code, no timeclock, and no way to come on shift.
-- This row type exists for future driver-app onboarding only.
-- 📌 MVP RULE: every assignable driver MUST have an employee_profiles row
-- with role = DRIVER and use the timeclock to come on shift.
-- Standalone driver accounts (users.role = DRIVER) have been removed from launch schema.
-- If a dedicated driver app is built later, a DRIVER value can be added to user_role via migration. Until then, all drivers are STAFF users with employee_profiles.role = DRIVER. They cannot
-- use standalone driver auth. To be unambiguous:

-- be assigned deliveries until the driver app is built. To be unambiguous: MVP drivers are operationally employee-backed and managed through staff flows (timeclock, POS WiFi, 5-digit code). Standalone DRIVER auth and a first-class driver app are a future milestone. Backend and auth code must not conflate these two models — build for the employee-driver path now, and add the standalone-driver path as an additive layer later.
-- Note: id column REMOVED. PK is user_id, matching customer_profiles and driver_profiles pattern.
-- This eliminates the naming drift between identity model and schema tables.
employee_user_id FK employee_profiles(user_id) -- was: employee_id FK employees(id)
employee_user_id FK employee_profiles(user_id) -- was: employee_id FK employees(id)
event_type ENUM: SALE_CASH | REFUND_CASH | NO_SALE_OPEN | SHIFT_OPEN | SHIFT_CLOSE | PAID_OUT
employee_user_id FK employee_profiles(user_id) -- was: employee_id FK employees(id)
device_id FK devices(id) nullable -- explicitly stored; device_id + printer_name together = full traceability
printer_name text -- OS printer name (kept for fallback if device deregistered)
location_id FK locations(id) -- REQUIRED: explicitly stored, not derived
ticket_type ENUM: WRONG_ITEM | MISSING_ITEM | COLD_FOOD | DELIVERY_ISSUE | DRIVER_ISSUE | QUALITY_ISSUE | PAYMENT_ISSUE | OTHER
-- DRIVER_ISSUE: rude, unprofessional, safety concern about the driver
status ENUM: OPEN | IN_REVIEW | WAITING_ON_CUSTOMER | RESOLVED | CLOSED
subject text NOT NULL -- short operator/customer-facing summary
created_source text NOT NULL -- CUSTOMER | STAFF | AUTO_OVERDUE

-- resolution_type: NULLABLE SUMMARY FIELD (ticket_resolution_type enum). NULL = no resolution recorded yet. NO_ACTION is a real business decision, not a placeholder. Uses the same canonical enum as support_ticket_resolutions. Reflects latest/primary resolution action for filtering, badges, dashboards. Source of truth for per-action detail = support_ticket_resolutions rows. See §25.2C for canonical vocabulary.
-- A ticket can have multiple resolution actions (e.g. credit + replacement)
-- Each action is a separate row in support_ticket_resolutions
-- Reopen: change status from RESOLVED back to OPEN | IN_REVIEW (allowed)
-- Reopen from CLOSED: NOT allowed. CLOSED is permanent. A new ticket must be opened instead.
-- Every status change must write a support_ticket_events row (see below)
support_ticket_resolutions (one row per resolution action on a ticket)
resolution_type ENUM: NO_ACTION | STORE_CREDIT | PARTIAL_REFUND | FULL_REFUND | REPLACEMENT | FOLLOW_UP
refund_request_id FK refund_requests(id) nullable -- set when resolution_type = PARTIAL/FULL_REFUND
replacement_order_id FK orders(id) nullable -- set when resolution_type = REPLACEMENT
credit_amount_cents int nullable -- set when resolution_type = STORE_CREDIT
-- One ticket can have multiple rows here (e.g. STORE_CREDIT row + REPLACEMENT row)
-- Constraint enforced by app layer: FULL_REFUND + REPLACEMENT not allowed on same ticket
-- FULL_REFUND + STORE_CREDIT not allowed on same ticket
-- ⚠️ FINANCIAL INVARIANT — Dual Enforcement Required (this is a money-control rule, not just a convention)
-- Layer 1 (DB): Add a partial unique index to prevent duplicate full-refund resolutions:
-- CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_one_full_refund_per_ticket ON support_ticket_resolutions(ticket_id)
-- This prevents two FULL_REFUND rows on one ticket at the DB level.
-- Layer 2 (Service): Wrap all resolution inserts in a transaction that queries existing rows
-- and rejects forbidden combos (FULL_REFUND + REPLACEMENT, FULL_REFUND + STORE_CREDIT).
-- Integration tests: must cover every forbidden pair and every allowed pair. This is a money leak if broken.
event_type ENUM: STATUS_CHANGE | PRIORITY_CHANGE | RESOLUTION_SET | REOPENED | NOTE_ADDED
from_value text nullable -- previous status/resolution value
performed_by FK users(id) nullable -- null for system-generated events (auto-close, auto-resolve); use event_type to identify SYSTEM actions
-- Every status mutation on a ticket must write here in addition to updating the ticket row.
-- ⚠️ from_value / to_value are plain text, not typed enums. This is a deliberate trade-off:

-- This table logs multiple event types (status, resolution) with different value domains. PRIORITY_CHANGE is retained for forward compatibility but priority is not a launch-scope column on support_tickets.
-- Treat these as descriptive audit fields, not canonical typed state. Use support_tickets.status
-- use this table for history only. priority is not a launch-scope field on support_tickets; if added later, this event table already supports tracking changes.
message_body text NOT NULL -- max 2000 chars enforced at API layer; HTML stripped server-side
is_internal_note boolean default false -- internal staff-only notes
-- Max length: 2000 characters. Enforce at API layer, not just frontend.
-- HTML stripping: strip all HTML tags on ingest. Store as plain text only.
-- No file attachments in MVP. Define file policy (type/size/scan) before enabling.
-- PII in staff-only notes: internal notes (is_internal_note = true) are not customer-visible
-- but are stored and auditable by admin.
Build in this sequence to avoid fragile dependencies:
2️⃣ Menu catalog: menu_categories, menu_items, modifier_groups, modifier_options
3️⃣ Orders + order_items snapshots + order_status_events + ETA fields
10️⃣ Driver profiles + full assignment flow + ratings + tips
14️⃣ Walk-in orders + in-store POS + employee codes
15️⃣ Cash register + receipt printing + drawer control

This table was referenced throughout the PRD but never properly defined. Every critical admin, manager, staff, or system action that changes data must write a row here. Despite the table name admin_audit_logs, it stores actions from all roles — ADMIN, MANAGER, KITCHEN, CASHIER, and SYSTEM. The name is kept for backward compatibility; the intent is a universal compliance trail. admin_audit_logs is the cross-domain compliance trail for sensitive actions. Domain-specific event tables (order_status_events, support_ticket_events, order_eta_events, order_driver_events, order_payments, customer_credit_ledger) remain the source of truth for detailed lifecycle history within their domains. Do not treat admin_audit_logs as a replacement for domain event tables — both are required.

When an action is system-generated (auto-accept, auto-close ticket, auto-resolve overdue ticket, scheduled expiry jobs), set performed_by_user_id = NULL and use the source/event_type field to record SYSTEM as the actor. Do NOT create a fake “system user” row in the users table. NULL performed_by + source = SYSTEM is the canonical pattern across all event tables. Every event table that can receive system-generated writes must have performed_by as nullable.
admin_audit_logs (universal compliance trail — not admin-only despite the name)
performed_by_user_id FK users(id) nullable -- null for SYSTEM-generated actions (auto-close, auto-resolve, scheduled jobs); non-null for all human-initiated actions
location_id FK locations(id) -- REQUIRED: explicitly stored
action text -- e.g. ORDER_CANCEL, POINTS_ADJUST, CREDIT_ISSUE, PUNCH_EDIT
target_type ENUM: ORDER | CUSTOMER | EMPLOYEE | DRIVER | INVENTORY | PROMO | REGISTER | SUPPORT_TICKET
target_id UUID -- id of the affected record
before JSONB nullable -- state before the change (snapshot)
after JSONB nullable -- state after the change (snapshot)
Actions that must write to admin_audit_logs:

Roles are stored on the users table (role ENUM). Permissions are enforced server-side based on role. There is no separate permissions table for MVP — role-based rules are hardcoded in the application layer. A feature-flag permissions table can be added later when fine-grained access control is needed.
Full access. All actions. All data. All settings.

All KDS actions (accept, request cancellation with reason, set preparing, set ready, no-show, ETA adjust, initiate refund requests, assign driver, approve busy-driver override, start delivery, mark delivered via PIN flow, mark no-show delivery — these are available to all KDS employees; cancellation requests and refund requests require Admin approval). Additional manager/admin capabilities: Approve no-sale drawer open. Edit employee punches. Override customer prepayment_required flag (with reason, logged in audit). Approve/reject add-item change requests (Section 13). Issue store credits up to a configured limit. Cannot approve cancellation or issue original-payment refund directly — Admin only.
Create walk-in orders. Apply approved coupon codes. Clock in/out. View their own orders only.

Full KDS operational actions: accept, reject/request cancel, set preparing, set ready, no-show, ETA adjust, assign driver, approve busy-driver override, start/complete delivery, enter delivery PIN, initiate refund request. Cannot approve cancellation or issue refunds (Admin only). Cannot access financial data or customer profiles.
View assigned deliveries (future driver app only). No access to admin panel.
Every API endpoint that performs a sensitive action must check role on the authenticated users row.
Frontend role-hiding is UX only. The backend must never trust the frontend's claim of what role a user is.
If you want auditability over which device printed what, and to support multiple stations reliably, add a devices table. This also allows per-station printer assignment without hardcoding.
type ENUM: KDS_SCREEN | POS_TERMINAL | RECEIPT_PRINTER | CASH_DRAWER | TIMECLOCK_TERMINAL -- Canonical set frozen in §24.7. Do not invent synonyms.
station_code text nullable -- matches registers.station_code if applicable
printer_name text nullable -- OS printer name used by the local print agent
last_seen_at timestamptz nullable -- updated by local print agent heartbeat

The guest checkout identity strategy is fully defined in Section 10.0.1. Summary: guest checkout creates a users row + user_identities row immediately via phone OTP. No temp guest tables. No orphaned records. All no-show counters and prepayment flags apply from the first order.
10.27 Inventory — Current Scope & Future Recipe Mapping
Admin manually adjusts stock quantities. The system does NOT automatically decrement inventory when an order is placed or completed.
This is intentional for MVP. True stock sync requires:
• Recipe / ingredient mappings (e.g. "1 lb wings order uses 1 lb of raw wings + 2 oz sauce")
• Auto-decrement trigger on order COMPLETED / PICKED_UP / DELIVERED
Without recipe mappings, auto-decrement is not meaningful. Build the manual system first, stabilise it, then add recipes as a phase 2 feature.
Future schema additions for recipe mapping (do not build yet):
quantity_used numeric -- how much of inventory_item is used per 1 unit of menu_item
Tracks all refund requests for paid cancelled orders. Admin approves or rejects. Keeps payment lifecycle clean and auditable.
support_ticket_id FK support_tickets(id) nullable -- null for auto-refund on cancel; set for ticket-driven refunds
initiated_by_user_id FK users(id) nullable -- null for system-auto-created refunds (e.g. paid order cancelled)
initiation_source ENUM: SYSTEM | ADMIN | MANAGER | KDS_EMPLOYEE -- who/what created the request; SYSTEM for auto-created on cancel; KDS_EMPLOYEE for any KDS-initiated request
status ENUM: PENDING | APPROVED | ISSUED | REJECTED | FAILED
notes text nullable -- admin internal notes
location_id FK locations(id) -- for multi-location audit
-- Rule 1: cancelled order with net_paid_amount_cents > 0 → refund_request created or existing open request updated for remaining refundable amount (support_ticket_id = NULL)
-- Rule 2: support ticket resolved as PARTIAL/FULL_REFUND → refund_request created with support_ticket_id set
-- Rule 3: admin NEVER bypasses refund_request to issue refund directly from ticket
-- ticket resolution triggers creation of a refund_request, not a direct payment action
-- Rule 4: one order can have multiple refund_requests (e.g. cancel refund + ticket partial refund)
-- Cumulative ISSUED refund amount across all refund_requests for an order
-- must NEVER exceed the order's net_paid_amount_cents.
-- Checked transactionally before approving/issuing any new refund:
-- SUM(amount_cents) FROM refund_requests WHERE order_id = X AND status IN (APPROVED, ISSUED)
-- WAIVED orders (net_paid = 0) can never receive a monetary refund.
-- This is the over-refund guard. If violated, real money leaks.
Stores the 4-digit delivery PIN generated per order and the verification result. Used to confirm the driver delivered to the correct recipient.
pin_hash text -- bcrypt/hash of 4-digit PIN; never store raw
expires_at timestamptz -- PIN should expire if delivery takes too long
failed_attempts int default 0 -- incremented on each mismatch; locked when >= 5
locked_at timestamptz nullable -- set when failed_attempts reaches limit
verified_by_user_id FK users(id) nullable -- KDS employee who entered PIN
verification_result ENUM: PENDING | VERIFIED | BYPASSED | EXPIRED | LOCKED
bypass_reason text nullable -- required if KDS employee bypasses PIN
Session persistence for secure login. The PRD references auth throughout but the schema was missing refresh token storage. This closes that gap.
refresh_token_hash text -- bcrypt hash of refresh token; never store raw token
device_info text nullable -- browser/device fingerprint for display
location_id FK locations(id) nullable -- null for non-POS sessions
is_pos_session boolean default false -- true if session created via POS WiFi login
revoked_by_user_id FK users(id) nullable -- for logout-all / admin revoke
-- Index: (user_id, revoked_at) for fast active session lookup
• Refresh tokens are hashed before storage. Raw token is never persisted.
• POS sessions (is_pos_session = true) are tied to the store IP range.
• Admin can revoke all sessions for a user (e.g. after account flag or suspicious activity).
• Sessions expire automatically. Short-lived access tokens (15 min); longer refresh tokens (7 days).

Moved from Enhancement Backlog to active schema. Lunch specials and time-restricted items must be enforced at launch — not manually toggled. Without this, "Lunch Specials" items appear all day or require staff to manually hide them every afternoon.
day_of_week ENUM: MON | TUE | WED | THU | FRI | SAT | SUN | ALL
-- INSERT INTO menu_item_schedules (menu_item_id, day_of_week, time_from, time_to)
• At checkout and on the menu page: items with a schedule are only visible and orderable within their defined windows.
• Outside the window: item card is greyed out with label "Available 11 AM – 3 PM".
• Backend validates schedule at order placement — frontend visibility is convenience only.

Every operational table must include location_id so data can be filtered, reported, and segregated by store. This ensures the shared DB supports multiple locations from day one without schema changes when location 2 opens.
Tables that MUST have location_id (canonical list):
Tables that are location-agnostic (do NOT need location_id):
• users, user_identities, customer_profiles (customers are shared across locations)
• promo_codes (promos are configured per code, not per location)
